steps.text: Conduct first hour PM emissions measurements using a separate ASTM E2515-11 sampling train operated concurrently with the paired ASTM E2515-11 sampling trains.
Conduct leak checks during the test run.
Perform post-test leak check procedures.
Prepare the emissions test report, ensuring it includes all data necessary to assess and assure quality and appropriately describes and identifies the specific unit covered.

constraints.text: Do not include sample volume collected during the process of conducting leak checks during a test run in the overall sampling volume.
Do not average the particulate matter (PM) results from a non-leaking sampling system with those from a leaking sampling system.

steps.text: Conduct first hour PM emissions measurements using a separate ASTM E2515–11 sampling train operated concurrently with the paired ASTM E2515–11 sampling trains used in compliance PM sampling.
Calculate CO emissions using the corrected equation 23 in section 13.8 of method 28WHH, summing CO emissions over four test categories instead of three.

constraints.text: Do not include sample volume collected during leak checks in the overall sampling volume to prevent dilution.

steps.text: Test the diluent component of the PEMS.
Conduct the relative accuracy test audit (RATA) as specified in section 8.2.
Conduct a relative accuracy audit (RAA) on the PEMS.

preconditions.text: The PEMS contains a diluent component.

constraints.text: The RAA is required on all PEMS, not just those classified as compliance PEMS.

steps.text: Amend appendix A–3 to part 60 by revising figure 4–3 under the heading ‘‘18.0 Tables, Diagrams, Flowcharts, and Validation Data’’ in method 4.
Check, record, and quantify the CD at two concentration values at least once daily (approximately 24 hours) in accordance with the method prescribed by the manufacturer.
Introduce the reference gas prior to any sample conditioning or filtration equipment.
Ensure that the reference gas passes through all filters, scrubbers, conditioners, and other monitor components used during normal sampling.
Ensure the reference gas passes through as much of the sampling probe as practical.
Adjust the CEMS calibration.

preconditions.text: Whenever the daily zero (or low-level) CD or the daily high-level CD exceeds two times the limits of the applicable PS’s in appendix B to this part

steps.text: Use equation 1-1 of this method to calculate the accuracy for the RAA.

preconditions.text: Unless specified otherwise in the applicable subpart of this part

constraints.text: The RAA must be calculated in the units of the applicable emission standard.
For the RAA, the excessive inaccuracy criterion is ±15 percent of the three-run average or ±7.5 percent of the applicable standard, whichever is greater.
For the RATA, the allowable RA is in the applicable PS in appendix B to this part.

For the CGA, for pollutant monitors, the audit inaccuracy must be ±15 percent of the average audit value as calculated using equation 1–1 of this method or the difference between the average CEMS response and the average audit value must be less than the specified alternative CGA criteria (±5 ppm for ≥50 ppm, ±3 ppm for >20 ppm but ≤50 ppm, +2 ppm for ≤20 ppm).
For diluent monitors, the CGA audit inaccuracy must be ±15 percent of the average audit value.

steps.text: At least once every 7 operating days, use a single mid- or high-level oxidized Hg reference gas to assess transport and measurement of oxidized mercury.
Follow the RATA test procedures in section 8.5 of PS 12A in appendix B to this part, using only three test runs, to conduct a Relative Accuracy Audit (RAA).
Calculate the relative accuracy according to equation 1–1 of Procedure 1 of this appendix.

preconditions.text: A RAA may be conducted in three of four calendar quarters, but in no more than three quarters in succession, as an alternative to the QGA.

constraints.text: The absolute value of the difference between the Hg CEMS output response and the reference gas must be less than or equal to 10.0 percent of the reference gas value or 0.8 mg/scm.

steps.text: Measure THC at the inlet and the outlet of the control device.
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the exhaust of the stationary RICE.
Determine the O2 concentration of the stationary RICE exhaust at the sampling port location.

preconditions.text: If demonstrating compliance with the THC percent reduction requirement

constraints.text: For Method 320 or 323 of 40 CFR part 63, appendix A, or ASTM D6348–03, the percent R must be greater than or equal to 70 and less than or equal to 130.
Formaldehyde concentration must be at 15 percent O2, dry basis. Results of this test consist of the average of the three 1-hour or longer runs.
THC concentration must be at 15 percent O2, dry basis. Results of this test consist of the average of the three 1-hour or longer runs.

For formaldehyde, CO, O2, and moisture measurement, ducts ≤6 inches in diameter may be sampled at a single point located at the duct centroid and ducts >6 and ≤12 inches in diameter may be sampled at 3 traverse points located at 16.7, 50.0, and 83.3% of the measurement line.

If the duct is >12 inches in diameter and the sampling port location meets the two and half-diameter criterion of section 11.1.1 of method 1 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A, the duct may be sampled at ‘3-point long line’; otherwise, conduct the stratification testing and select sampling points according to section 8.1.2 of method 7E of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A.
If using a control device, the sampling site must be located at the outlet of the control device.

steps.text: Check gauge calibration quarterly.
Check transducer calibration monthly.
Determine the emission capture system efficiency.

preconditions.text: The capture system must meet the criteria in method 204 of appendix M to 40 CFR part 51 for a permanent total enclosure (PE) and direct all the exhaust gases from the enclosure to an add-on control device.

constraints.text: Use a pressure sensor with measurement sensitivity of 0.002 inch water.
CO concentration must be at 15 percent O2, dry basis.
Results of this test consist of the average of the three 1-hour or longer runs.

steps.text: Contact the person listed in the FOR FURTHER INFORMATION CONTACT section to schedule your inspection.
Access publicly available docket materials either electronically through www.regulations.gov or in hard copy at the Air Regulatory Management Section.

constraints.text: The Regional Office’s official hours of business are Monday through Friday 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m., excluding Federal holidays.
